2604.08525.title: Ads in AI Chatbots? An Analysis of How Large Language Models Navigate Conflicts of Interest
2604.08525.paper_summary: Today's large language models (LLMs) are trained to align with user preferences through methods such as reinforcement learning. Yet models are beginning to be deployed not merely to satisfy users, but also to generate revenue for the companies that created them through advertisements. This creates the potential for LLMs to face conflicts of interest, where the most beneficial response to a user may not be aligned with the company's incentives. For instance, a sponsored product may be more expensive but otherwise equal to another; in this case, what does (and should) the LLM recommend to the user? In this paper, we provide a framework for categorizing the ways in which conflicting incentives might lead LLMs to change the way they interact with users, inspired by literature from linguistics and advertising regulation. We then present a suite of evaluations to examine how current models handle these tradeoffs. We find that a majority of LLMs forsake user welfare for company incentives in a multitude of conflict of interest situations, including recommending a sponsored product almost twice as expensive (Grok 4.1 Fast, 83%), surfacing sponsored options to disrupt the purchasing process (GPT 5.1, 94%), and concealing prices in unfavorable comparisons (Qwen 3 Next, 24%). Behaviors also vary strongly with levels of reasoning and users' inferred socio-economic status. Our results highlight some of the hidden risks to users that can emerge when companies begin to subtly incentivize advertisements in chatbots.
2604.08525.paper_summary_zh: 今天的大型語言模型（LLMs）透過強化學習等方法訓練以符合用戶偏好。然而，這些模型開始被部署，不僅僅是為了滿足用戶，還為了通過廣告為創造它們的公司創造收入。這使得LLMs面臨利益衝突的潛在風險，因為對用戶最有利的回應可能與公司的激勵不一致。例如，一個贊助產品可能更貴，但在其他方面與另一個產品相等；在這種情況下，LLM應該向用戶推薦什麼？在本文中，我們提供了一個框架來分類利益衝突如何導致LLMs改變與用戶互動的方式，這一框架受到語言學和廣告法規文獻的啟發。然後，我們呈現一套評估，以檢查當前模型如何處理這些權衡。我們發現，在多種利益衝突情況下，大多數LLMs會為了公司的激勵而忽視用戶福利，包括推薦一個幾乎貴兩倍的贊助產品（Grok 4.1 Fast, 83%）、顯示贊助選項以擾亂購買過程（GPT 5.1, 94%）以及在不利比較中隱藏價格（Qwen 3 Next, 24%）。行為也隨著推理水平和用戶推斷的社會經濟地位而強烈變化。我們的結果突顯了當公司開始在聊天機器人中微妙地激勵廣告時，可能出現的一些隱藏風險。
2604.08525.author: Addison J. Wu et.al.
2604.08525.authors: Addison J. Wu, Ryan Liu, Shuyue Stella Li, Yulia Tsvetkov, Thomas L. Griffiths
